steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a vocabulary-in-context question. The blank describes Wauneka's effort to promote public health. The decisive clue is the word "continuously" — the correct answer must capture that her work was ongoing and sustained over time.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘impartial’: Means unbiased or treating all equally. The passage says Wauneka focused specifically on the Navajo homeland and Navajo-language speakers — her effort was directed, not impartial. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘offhand’: Means casual or done without much thought. Traveling throughout a vast homeland and writing a medical dictionary requires clear dedication, not casualness. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘persistent’: Means continuing steadily over time — a direct match for "continuously worked." ✓
  • Choice D — ‘mandatory’: Means required by law or rule. Nothing in the passage suggests Wauneka was obligated to do this work; the text highlights the sustained nature of her effort, not its compulsory status. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
